issue_description: Customer requested to combine two beneficiaries into a cleaner list for convenience, with no change to the actual payments made.
Customer noticed an installment plan was split across two authorisations and was advised this is expected for that product type.
Customer’s documentation submission included edited fields on a bank letter, and the bank confirmation could not validate the modified content.

Merchant reported a seasonal sales cycle, but observed monthly totals did not match the stated seasonality and were unexplained.
Customer requested waiver of standard documentation for cross-border transfers to multiple recipients.

Account servicing processed a legal name change after receiving the appropriate documentation and verified the details.
The customer’s profile indicated retail activity, yet transfer sizes were consistent with wholesale trade.
The customer requested payment to a personal account despite providing a corporate beneficiary agreement.
A payment reversal took longer than expected because it was batch-processed, and the expected timeline was communicated.
A cash withdrawal dispute was opened because of a late receipt, and the investigation found the withdrawal completed normally.
Counterparty refused to share contract terms but requested payment in advance.
Account activity shifted from low value to frequent high value after a change in device/location.
Merchant onboarding required a minor correction to store hours, which was updated without impacting existing transactions.
